questions.question: What is the initial state in React?
options: A) A constant state
B) A state that can change
C) A state that is passed as props
D) A state that is set by default
questions.explanation: The initial state in React is a state that is set by default when the component mounts.

questions.question: How do you update state in a React component?
options: A) Using the setState function
B) Using the useState hook
C) Using the console.log function
D) Using the document.getElementById function
questions.explanation: The useState hook is used to update state in a React component.

questions.question: Why do you need to lift the state up in a React application?
options: A) So that the state can be shared between components
B) So that the state can be initialized asynchronously
C) So that the state can be updated by multiple components
D) So that the state can be accessed from the parent component
questions.explanation: Lifting the state up is necessary when you want to share state between components or when you want to make it easier to manage state consistency in your application.

questions.question: What is the difference between a stateless and a stateful component in React?
options: A) A stateless component is a component that can update its own state
B) A stateless component is a component that cannot update its own state
C) A stateless component is a component that can only receive props
D) A stateless component is a component that can only send props to other components
questions.explanation: A stateless component is a component that cannot update its own state, whereas a stateful component can update its own state.

questions.question: What is event handling in React?
options: A) The process of updating state when a user interacts with the application
B) The process of rendering components when the application mounts
C) The process of sharing state between components
D) The process of appending CSS styles to a component
questions.explanation: Event handling in React is the process of updating state when a user interacts with the application.
